pub fn process_execute_transaction(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let governance_data = get_governance_data(program_id, governance_info)?;
    let mut proposal_data = get_proposal_data_for_governance(program_id, proposal_info, governance_info.key)?;
    let mut proposal_transaction_data = get_proposal_transaction_data_for_proposal(
        program_id, proposal_transaction_info, proposal_info.key)?;

    proposal_data.assert_can_execute_transaction(
        &proposal_transaction_data, &governance_data.config, clock.unix_timestamp)?;

    let instructions = proposal_transaction_data.instructions.iter().map(Instruction::from);
    let instruction_account_infos = account_info_iter.as_slice();

    let mut governance_seeds = governance_data.get_governance_address_seeds()?.to_vec();
    let (_, bump_seed) = Pubkey::find_program_address(&governance_seeds, program_id);
    governance_seeds.push(&[bump_seed]);

    for instruction in instructions {
        invoke_signed(&instruction, instruction_account_infos, &signers_seeds[..])?;
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls - MITIGATED
invoke_signed uses ? for error propagation in each iteration. If any instruction fails, the entire transaction reverts. Governance PDA signing uses find_program_address for canonical bump.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept assert_can_execute_transaction (L48-52), invoke_signed with ? in loop (L72), find_program_address for signing (L65-68)

pub fn process_execute_transaction(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let proposal_transaction_data = get_proposal_transaction_data_for_proposal(
        program_id, proposal_transaction_info, proposal_info.key)?;

    let instructions = proposal_transaction_data.instructions.iter().map(Instruction::from);
    let instruction_account_infos = account_info_iter.as_slice();

    let governance_seeds = governance_data.get_governance_address_seeds()?.to_vec();

    for instruction in instructions {
        let _ = invoke_signed(&instruction, instruction_account_infos, &signers_seeds[..]);
    }

    proposal_transaction_data.execution_status = TransactionExecutionStatus::Success;
    proposal_transaction_data.serialize(&mut proposal_transaction_info.data.borrow_mut()[..])?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls
CRITICAL
CPI errors are silently discarded with 'let _ ='. Failed instructions are still marked as Success. No execution eligibility check — any proposal transaction can be executed regardless of state. Governance decisions may be recorded as executed without actually completing.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - removed assert_can_execute_transaction (L48-52), replaced invoke_signed ? with 'let _ =' (L72), marks Success regardless of actual result

pub fn process_create_realm(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    name: String,
    realm_config_args: RealmConfigArgs,
) -> ProgramResult {
    if !realm_info.data_is_empty() {
        return Err(GovernanceError::RealmAlreadyExists.into());
    }
    assert_valid_realm_config_args(&realm_config_args)?;

    create_spl_token_account_signed(
        payer_info, governance_token_holding_info,
        &get_governing_token_holding_address_seeds(realm_info.key, governance_token_mint_info.key),
        governance_token_mint_info, realm_info, program_id,
        system_info, spl_token_info, rent_sysvar_info, rent,
    )?;

    create_and_serialize_account_signed::<RealmConfigAccount>(
        payer_info, realm_config_info, &realm_config_data,
        &get_realm_config_address_seeds(realm_info.key),
        program_id, system_info, rent, 0,
    )?;

    create_and_serialize_account_signed::<RealmV2>(
        payer_info, realm_info, &realm_data,
        &get_realm_address_seeds(&name),
        program_id, system_info, rent, 0,
    )?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls - MITIGATED
All three account creation CPIs use ? for error propagation. Realm existence check prevents re-creation. Config args validated before any CPI.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept three create_and_serialize/create_spl_token_account_signed calls all with ? propagation, realm existence check (L52-54), config validation (L56)

pub fn process_deposit_governing_tokens(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    amount: u64,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let realm_data = get_realm_data(program_id, realm_info)?;
    realm_data.assert_is_valid_governing_token_mint_and_holding(
        program_id, realm_info.key, &governing_token_mint, governing_token_holding_info.key)?;

    if is_spl_token_account(governing_token_source_info) {
        transfer_spl_tokens(
            governing_token_source_info, governing_token_holding_info,
            governing_token_source_authority_info, amount, spl_token_info,
        )?;
    } else if is_spl_token_mint(governing_token_source_info) {
        mint_spl_tokens_to(
            governing_token_source_info, governing_token_holding_info,
            governing_token_source_authority_info, amount, spl_token_info,
        )?;
    } else {
        return Err(GovernanceError::InvalidGoverningTokenSource.into());
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls - MITIGATED
Both transfer and mint paths use ? for error propagation. Source type is validated and unknown types rejected. Mint and holding account validated against realm state before any CPI.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept transfer_spl_tokens/mint_spl_tokens_to with ? (L73-86), mint/holding validation (L60-65), source type branching with error fallback (L87-89)

pub fn process_withdraw_governing_tokens(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
) -> ProgramResult {
    if !governing_token_owner_info.is_signer {
        return Err(GovernanceError::GoverningTokenOwnerMustSign.into());
    }

    let realm_data = get_realm_data(program_id, realm_info)?;
    realm_data.assert_is_valid_governing_token_mint_and_holding(
        program_id, realm_info.key, &governing_token_mint, governing_token_holding_info.key)?;

    token_owner_record_data.assert_can_withdraw_governing_tokens(clock.unix_timestamp)?;

    transfer_spl_tokens_signed(
        governing_token_holding_info, governing_token_destination_info,
        realm_info, &get_realm_address_seeds(&realm_data.name),
        program_id, token_owner_record_data.governing_token_deposit_amount, spl_token_info,
    )?;

    token_owner_record_data.governing_token_deposit_amount = 0;
    token_owner_record_data.serialize(&mut token_owner_record_info.data.borrow_mut()[..])?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls - MITIGATED
transfer_spl_tokens_signed uses ? for error propagation. Signer check, mint validation, and withdrawal eligibility all verified before CPI. Deposit amount zeroed after successful transfer.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept signer check (L41-43), mint/holding validation, withdraw eligibility check, transfer_spl_tokens_signed with ? (L73-81), deposit zeroing (L83)

pub fn process_execute_transaction(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let governance_data = get_governance_data(program_id, governance_info)?;
    let mut proposal_data = get_proposal_data_for_governance(
        program_id, proposal_info, governance_info.key)?;
    let mut proposal_transaction_data = get_proposal_transaction_data_for_proposal(
        program_id, proposal_transaction_info, proposal_info.key)?;

    // Validate execution eligibility BEFORE any CPI
    proposal_data.assert_can_execute_transaction(
        &proposal_transaction_data, &governance_data.config, clock.unix_timestamp)?;

    let instructions = proposal_transaction_data.instructions.iter().map(Instruction::from);

    for instruction in instructions {
        invoke_signed(&instruction, instruction_account_infos, &signers_seeds[..])?;
    }

    // Update state AFTER CPI
    proposal_transaction_data.executed_at = Some(clock.unix_timestamp);
    proposal_transaction_data.execution_status = TransactionExecutionStatus::Success;
    proposal_transaction_data.serialize(&mut proposal_transaction_info.data.borrow_mut()[..])?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks - MITIGATED
Execution eligibility validated before any CPI. State updates (executed_at, execution_status) written after all instructions complete. Even during CPI callback, the transaction data still shows unexecuted, so re-execution check would fail.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept assert_can_execute_transaction BEFORE CPI (L48-52), state updates AFTER CPI (L96-107)

pub fn process_execute_transaction(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let mut proposal_transaction_data = get_proposal_transaction_data_for_proposal(
        program_id, proposal_transaction_info, proposal_info.key)?;

    // WRONG: Mark as executed BEFORE running instructions
    proposal_transaction_data.executed_at = Some(clock.unix_timestamp);
    proposal_transaction_data.execution_status = TransactionExecutionStatus::Success;
    proposal_transaction_data.serialize(&mut proposal_transaction_info.data.borrow_mut()[..])?;

    let instructions = proposal_transaction_data.instructions.iter().map(Instruction::from);

    for instruction in instructions {
        invoke_signed(&instruction, instruction_account_infos, &signers_seeds[..])?;
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks
CRITICAL
Transaction marked as executed before instructions actually run. If any instruction fails, the state incorrectly shows Success. During CPI, the calling program could re-read the proposal and make decisions based on false execution status.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - moved state updates (originally L96-107) to before CPI loop (originally L72), removed assert_can_execute_transaction (L48-52)

pub fn process_deposit_governing_tokens(
    program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo], amount: u64,
) -> ProgramResult {
    // FIRST: Transfer tokens to holding
    transfer_spl_tokens(
        governing_token_source_info, governing_token_holding_info,
        governing_token_source_authority_info, amount, spl_token_info,
    )?;

    // THEN: Create or update TokenOwnerRecord
    if token_owner_record_info.data_is_empty() {
        if !governing_token_owner_info.is_signer {
            return Err(GovernanceError::GoverningTokenOwnerMustSign.into());
        }
        let token_owner_record_data = TokenOwnerRecordV2 {
            governing_token_deposit_amount: amount,
            // ...
        };
        create_and_serialize_account_signed(
            payer_info, token_owner_record_info, &token_owner_record_data,
            &token_owner_record_address_seeds, program_id, system_info, &rent, 0,
        )?;
    } else {
        let mut record = get_token_owner_record_data_for_seeds(
            program_id, token_owner_record_info, &token_owner_record_address_seeds)?;
        record.governing_token_deposit_amount = record.governing_token_deposit_amount
            .checked_add(amount).unwrap();
        record.serialize(&mut token_owner_record_info.data.borrow_mut()[..])?;
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks - MITIGATED
Tokens are transferred to holding account FIRST, then the TokenOwnerRecord is created or updated. During the transfer CPI, no record exists yet (for new deposits) or the amount hasn't been increased (for existing). Reentrancy during transfer cannot exploit the deposit.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept transfer-then-update CPI ordering: tokens transferred (L73-78) before record creation/update (L92-131)

) -> ProgramResult {
    token_owner_record_data.assert_can_withdraw_governing_tokens(clock.unix_timestamp)?;

    // Transfer full deposit amount
    transfer_spl_tokens_signed(
        governing_token_holding_info, governing_token_destination_info,
        realm_info, &get_realm_address_seeds(&realm_data.name),
        program_id, token_owner_record_data.governing_token_deposit_amount, spl_token_info,

    token_owner_record_data.governing_token_deposit_amount = 0;

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks - MITIGATED
Withdrawal eligibility checked first (locks, outstanding votes). Transfer executes before deposit is zeroed. Note: reentrancy during transfer could theoretically re-enter, but assert_can_withdraw checks prevent double-withdrawal since the amount hasn't changed yet.<|eot_id|>

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept withdraw eligibility check, transfer then zero pattern (transfer L73-81, zero L83-84)

pub fn process_cast_vote(
    program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo], vote: Vote,
) -> ProgramResult {
    if !vote_record_info.data_is_empty() {
        return Err(GovernanceError::VoteAlreadyExists.into());
    }

    let realm_data = get_realm_data_for_governing_token_mint(
        program_id, realm_info, vote_governing_token_mint_info.key)?;

    let mut proposal_data = get_proposal_data_for_governance_and_governing_mint(
        program_id, proposal_info, governance_info.key, &proposal_governing_token_mint)?;
    proposal_data.assert_can_cast_vote(&governance_data.config, &vote, clock.unix_timestamp)?;

    let mut voter_record = get_token_owner_record_data_for_realm_and_governing_mint(
        program_id, voter_token_owner_record_info, &governance_data.realm,
        vote_governing_token_mint_info.key)?;
    voter_record.assert_token_owner_or_delegate_is_signer(governance_authority_info)?;

    voter_record.unrelinquished_votes_count = voter_record
        .unrelinquished_votes_count.checked_add(1).unwrap();

    proposal_data.assert_valid_vote(&vote)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V10 - Denial of Service - MITIGATED
VoteAlreadyExists prevents double-voting (which would inflate vote weights). assert_can_cast_vote enforces voting window. assert_valid_vote rejects malformed votes. checked_add prevents vote count overflow.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept VoteAlreadyExists (L64-66), assert_can_cast_vote timing check, assert_valid_vote, checked_add for vote count (L109-111)

pub fn process_cast_vote(
    program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo], vote: Vote,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let mut proposal_data = get_proposal_data_for_governance_and_governing_mint(
        program_id, proposal_info, governance_info.key, &proposal_governing_token_mint)?;

    match &vote {
        Vote::Approve(choices) => {
            for (option, choice) in proposal_data.options.iter_mut().zip(choices) {
                option.vote_weight = option.vote_weight + choice.get_choice_weight(voter_weight)?;
            }
        }
        Vote::Deny => {
            proposal_data.deny_vote_weight = Some(
                proposal_data.deny_vote_weight.unwrap() + voter_weight);
        }
        _ => {}
    }

    proposal_data.serialize(&mut proposal_info.data.borrow_mut()[..])?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V10 - Denial of Service
CRITICAL
No double-vote prevention allows unlimited vote inflation. No voting window check allows voting after deadline. No signer check allows anyone to vote. Unchecked addition can overflow vote weights. Missing vote validation accepts malformed votes.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - removed VoteAlreadyExists check (L64-66), removed assert_can_cast_vote (L89), removed assert_token_owner_or_delegate_is_signer (L104), replaced checked_add with unchecked + (L120,131), removed assert_valid_vote (L114)

pub fn process_create_proposal(
    program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    name: String, vote_type: VoteType, options: Vec<String>,
) -> ProgramResult {
    if !proposal_info.data_is_empty() {
        return Err(GovernanceError::ProposalAlreadyExists.into());
    }

    let realm_data = get_realm_data_for_governing_token_mint(
        program_id, realm_info, governing_token_mint_info.key)?;
    let mut governance_data = get_governance_data_for_realm(
        program_id, governance_info, realm_info.key)?;

    let mut proposal_owner_record = get_token_owner_record_data_for_realm(
        program_id, proposal_owner_record_info, realm_info.key)?;
    proposal_owner_record.assert_token_owner_or_delegate_is_signer(governance_authority_info)?;

    proposal_owner_record.assert_can_create_proposal(
        &realm_data, &governance_data.config, voter_weight)?;

    proposal_owner_record.outstanding_proposal_count = proposal_owner_record
        .outstanding_proposal_count.checked_add(1).unwrap();

    assert_valid_proposal_options(&options, &vote_type)?;

    governance_data.active_proposal_count = governance_data
        .active_proposal_count.checked_add(1).unwrap();
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V10 - Denial of Service - MITIGATED
Proposal existence check prevents overwrites. Voter weight threshold prevents spam proposals. Outstanding proposal count limit prevents one user from flooding governance. checked_add prevents count overflow. Options validated against vote type.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept ProposalAlreadyExists (L62-63), assert_can_create_proposal weight/count check, checked_add for counts (L108-110, L164-166), assert_valid_proposal_options

Code:
pub fn process_execute_transaction(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let mut signers_seeds: Vec<&[&[u8]]> = vec![];

    // Sign using governance PDA
    let mut governance_seeds = governance_data.get_governance_address_seeds()?.to_vec();
    let (_, bump_seed) = Pubkey::find_program_address(&governance_seeds, program_id);
    let bump = &[bump_seed];
    governance_seeds.push(bump);
    signers_seeds.push(&governance_seeds[..]);

    // Sign using treasury PDA if needed
    let mut treasury_seeds = get_native_treasury_address_seeds(governance_info.key).to_vec();
    let (treasury_address, treasury_bump_seed) =
        Pubkey::find_program_address(&treasury_seeds, program_id);
    let treasury_bump = &[treasury_bump_seed];

    if instruction_account_infos.iter().any(|a| a.key == &treasury_address) {
        treasury_seeds.push(treasury_bump);
        signers_seeds.push(&treasury_seeds[..]);
    }

    for instruction in instructions {
        invoke_signed(&instruction, instruction_account_infos, &signers_seeds[..])?;
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V9 - Bump Seed Canonicalization - MITIGATED
Dual PDA signing with find_program_address for both governance and treasury. Canonical bumps derived at runtime. Treasury PDA only added to signers when required by the instruction accounts.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept dual find_program_address: governance (L65-68) and treasury (L74-76), conditional treasury signing (L79-82)

    create_spl_token_account_signed(
        payer_info, governance_token_holding_info,
        &get_governing_token_holding_address_seeds(realm_info.key, governance_token_mint_info.key),
        governance_token_mint_info, realm_info, program_id,
        system_info, spl_token_info, rent_sysvar_info, rent,
    )?;

    create_and_serialize_account_signed::<RealmConfigAccount>(
        payer_info, realm_config_info, &realm_config_data,
        &get_realm_config_address_seeds(realm_info.key),
        program_id, system_info, rent, 0,
    )?;

    create_and_serialize_account_signed::<RealmV2>(
        payer_info, realm_info, &realm_data,
        &get_realm_address_seeds(&name),
        program_id, system_info, rent, 0,
    )?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

pub fn process_create_proposal(
    program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    name: String, vote_type: VoteType, options: Vec<String>,
    use_deny_option: bool, proposal_seed: Pubkey,
) -> ProgramResult {
    if !proposal_info.data_is_empty() {
        return Err(GovernanceError::ProposalAlreadyExists.into());
    }

    let realm_data = get_realm_data_for_governing_token_mint(
        program_id, realm_info, governing_token_mint_info.key)?;

    governance_data.assert_governing_token_mint_can_vote(
        &realm_data, governing_token_mint_info.key, &VoteKind::Electorate)?;

    proposal_owner_record_data.assert_can_create_proposal(
        &realm_data, &governance_data.config, voter_weight)?;

    assert_valid_proposal_options(&options, &vote_type)?;

    let proposal_options: Vec<ProposalOption> = options.iter()
        .map(|o| ProposalOption { label: o.to_string(), vote_weight: 0,
            vote_result: OptionVoteResult::None, transactions_executed_count: 0,
            transactions_count: 0, transactions_next_index: 0 })
        .collect();
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE

Proposal existence prevents overwrites. Token mint validated for voting eligibility. Voter weight checked against minimum threshold. Proposal options validated against vote type (e.g., single-choice can't have multiple options).<|eot_id|>

pub fn process_set_realm_authority(
    program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    action: SetRealmAuthorityAction,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let realm_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let realm_authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    let mut realm_data = get_realm_data_for_authority(
        program_id, realm_info, realm_authority_info.key)?;

    if !realm_authority_info.is_signer {
        return Err(GovernanceError::RealmAuthorityMustSign.into());
    }

    let new_realm_authority = match action {
        SetRealmAuthorityAction::SetChecked => {
            let new_authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
            assert_governance_for_realm(program_id, new_authority_info, realm_info.key)?;
            Some(*new_authority_info.key)
        }
        SetRealmAuthorityAction::SetUnchecked => {
            Some(*next_account_info(account_info_iter)?.key)
        }
        SetRealmAuthorityAction::Remove => None,
    };

    realm_data.authority = new_realm_authority;
    realm_data.serialize(&mut realm_info.data.borrow_mut()[..])?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V1 - Access Control - MITIGATED
get_realm_data_for_authority validates current authority matches (L31-32). Signer check required (L34-36). SetChecked mode validates new authority is a governance from this realm via assert_governance_for_realm. Three action modes provide flexible but secure authority management.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: direct extraction

) -> ProgramResult {

        program_id, token_owner_record_info, &token_owner_record_address_seeds)?;

        .governing_token_deposit_amount

}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

checked_add prevents overflow when accumulating deposit amounts. Note: .unwrap() on checked_add will panic on overflow — while not ideal (should use ok_or), it prevents silent wraparound.<|eot_id|>

pub fn process_cast_vote(
    program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo], vote: Vote,
) -> ProgramResult {
    voter_token_owner_record_data.unrelinquished_votes_count = voter_token_owner_record_data
        .unrelinquished_votes_count.checked_add(1).unwrap();

    match &vote {
        Vote::Approve(choices) => {
            for (option, choice) in proposal_data.options.iter_mut().zip(choices) {
                option.vote_weight = option.vote_weight
                    .checked_add(choice.get_choice_weight(voter_weight)?).unwrap();
            }
        }
        Vote::Deny => {
            proposal_data.deny_vote_weight = Some(
                proposal_data.deny_vote_weight.unwrap()
                    .checked_add(voter_weight).unwrap());
        }
        Vote::Veto => {
            proposal_data.veto_vote_weight = proposal_data.veto_vote_weight
                .checked_add(voter_weight).unwrap();
        }
        Vote::Abstain => {
            return Err(GovernanceError::NotSupportedVoteType.into());
        }
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V8 - Integer Overflow - MITIGATED
checked_add used for all vote weight accumulations and vote count increments. Prevents vote weight overflow attacks. Abstain explicitly rejected.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept checked_add for vote count (L109-111), vote weight accumulation for Approve (L120-122), Deny (L128-131), Veto (L133-135)

pub fn process_execute_transaction(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let option = &mut proposal_data.options[proposal_transaction_data.option_index as usize];
    option.transactions_executed_count = option.transactions_executed_count
        .checked_add(1).unwrap();

    if proposal_data.options.iter()
        .filter(|o| o.vote_result == OptionVoteResult::Succeeded)
        .all(|o| o.transactions_executed_count == o.transactions_count)
    {
        proposal_data.closed_at = Some(clock.unix_timestamp);
        proposal_data.state = ProposalState::Completed;
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V8 - Integer Overflow - MITIGATED
checked_add for transaction execution count. Completion detection compares executed_count to transactions_count for all succeeded options, ensuring all must complete before marking Completed.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE
